# Atomic Note

> [!TIP]
> One idea per note. State clearly, provide evidence, then link related notes. Press `Ctrl+K` to search existing notes and `Alt+N` to create new atomic notes.

---

## Idea

**[State the core idea in one clear sentence]**

[Expand on the idea in 2-3 sentences. Explain what it means and why it matters. Write it in your own words, not copied from the source.]

## Source

**Where this idea came from:**
[Book title, article, conversation, or personal observation]

**Original context:**

> [Relevant quote or passage from the source material]

## Supporting Evidence

- [Fact, data point, or example that supports this idea]
- [Another piece of evidence]
- [Additional supporting detail]

> [!TIP]
> Strong atomic notes have at least 2-3 pieces of supporting evidence from different sources.

## Connections

Related notes and how they connect (use `Ctrl+K` to find related notes):

- **[Related note title]** — [How it connects to this idea]
- **[Related note title]** — [What relationship exists?]
- **[Related note title]** — [Does it support or contradict?]

**Example connection:**

- **Spaced repetition improves recall** — Supports the broader principle that active engagement with material strengthens understanding[^1]

## References

[^1]: [Author, Title, Year, or URL]
[^2]: [Second reference source]
